description: 'phialu' is water as a substance: the liquid in a cup or river, and the water moving through clouds, plants, and bodies. Salinity, cleanliness, and fitness for drinking are separate properties. 'larewu' includes liquids of every kind.
articulatory_notes: Bring the lips close together for unvoiced bilabial 'ph' and release the narrow breath into high-front unrounded 'i'. Keep the vowel stream open across the syllable boundary and lower the tongue into stressed central 'a'. Touch the tongue tip to the alveolar ridge for lateral 'l' and finish with high-back rounded 'u'.
sound_symbolism: Narrow breath opens into bright 'i', crosses the audible space before stressed 'a', then passes beside the tongue into rounded 'u'. The uninterrupted change of vowels and the lateral release give the word a liquid continuity.
usage_notes: 'phialu' usually behaves as a mass noun and can take quantifiers such as 'sheloi phialu' (much water). Specific amounts commonly use a container noun: 'ta lopha phialu' (one vessel of water). Watering or irrigation is expressed compositionally as 'phialu loa' (give water).

pillars.solarpunk-values: Water links a household cup to the local watershed. Rainfall, soil, farms, and wetlands all enter that relation. Plain description lets a community ask who has access, what has been contaminated, and what must be restored.
pillars.pre-industrial-wisdom: Wells, cisterns, and channels required close local knowledge and continuing care. So did seasonal flows and signs of unsafe water.

semantic_domains.nature: water across weather, waterways, soils, and living bodies
